question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseTimeout is used as a gRPC deadline — which is an absolute time, not a relative timeout — to cause clock skew between the importer and block node to make all status requests appear to time out, returning EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE for all nodes and halting block streaming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user send a stream with a single BlockItemSet containing exactly maxBlockItems+1 items to immediately trigger the BlockStreamException threshold, causing onError() to be called, and by repeating this maxSubscribeAttempts times, permanently deactivate the targeted block node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay default of 1 minute — combined with a small block node pool — to cause a denial of service where all nodes are simultaneously deactivated, and the 1-minute readmit window means no transactions are confirmed for at least 1 minute per attack cycle?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum bound on maxStreamResponseSize to cause the importer to allocate an excessively large gRPC inbound buffer — consuming all available memory — causing an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the importer process and halts all transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the errors counter is reset to 0 after deactivation (errors.set(0) in onError()) — meaning after readmit, the node gets a fresh maxSubscribeAttempts quota — to create a predictable attack cycle where the attacker deactivates each node exactly every maxSubscribeAttempts errors, maintaining a perpetual shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 400ms responseTimeout by introducing exactly 401ms of latency on the block node's status endpoint — via a network middlebox or proxy — causing all getBlockRange() calls to return EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE while the node appears operational, silently halting block selection and transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that pendingCount is accumulated across multiple BlockItemSets within a single block — not reset between sets — to craft a block with many small sets that individually appear valid but collectively exceed maxBlockItems, triggering BlockStreamException and node deactivation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay mechanism combined with a single-node block node configuration — where there is only one block node — to cause a complete and sustained network shutdown by keeping that single node permanently inactive through repeated error injection timed to the readmit window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize is validated with @NotNull but has no upper bound constraint — allowing it to be set to Long.MAX_VALUE bytes — causing the (int) cast in BlockNode constructor to overflow to a negative maxInboundMessageSize, which gRPC may interpret as unlimited or zero, causing unpredictable behavior that halts block streaming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxSubscribeAttempts is validated only at startup via @Validated — meaning runtime changes to the property (e.g., via Spring Cloud Config or actuator) bypass validation — to set maxSubscribeAttempts to 0 or negative, causing immediate deactivation of all block nodes on the first error?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseTimeout is validated with @DurationMin(millis = 100) but has no maximum bound — allowing it to be set to Duration.ofDays(365) — causing all status requests to block for up to a year, effectively freezing the block node selection process and halting all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxBlockItems is an int (not long) — with a maximum value of ~2.1 billion — to craft a stream where pendingCount is incremented to exactly Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing the comparison pendingCount > streamProperties.getMaxBlockItems() to behave incorrectly due to overflow, bypassing the limit and causing memory exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @DurationMin(seconds = 10) constraint on readmitDelay — which only validates the minimum — to set readmitDelay to Duration.ofDays(365), causing any deactivated block node to remain inactive for a year, and by triggering deactivation of all nodes, cause a year-long total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DataSizeUnit(DataUnit.MEGABYTES) annotation — which defaults numeric values to megabytes — to cause a configuration where maxStreamResponseSize is set to 0MB (if 0 passes @NotNull), resulting in a gRPC channel with maxInboundMessageSize of 0 that rejects all responses and halts block streaming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the errors counter is shared across all streaming attempts on a single BlockNode instance — meaning errors from unrelated causes (network blips, timeouts) accumulate toward the maxSubscribeAttempts threshold — to amplify the effect of transient network issues and cause premature node deactivation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseTimeout is used with withDeadlineAfter() — which creates an absolute deadline — to cause a scenario where high CPU load on the importer delays the deadline calculation, making the effective timeout shorter than responseTimeout and causing spurious EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE returns that prevent block streaming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the BlockStreamException thrown when maxBlockItems is exceeded propagates to onError() — which increments the error counter — to use oversized block injection as a reliable mechanism to increment the error counter exactly maxSubscribeAttempts times and deactivate every block node in the pool?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay combined with the fact that tryReadmit() checks Instant.now().isAfter(readmitTime.get()) — a time-based check — to perform a timing attack where error injection is precisely timed to occur within milliseconds of each readmit, ensuring nodes are never active long enough to process a block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize controls the gRPC channel's maxInboundMessageSize — which is set once at channel construction — to cause a scenario where the importer is configured with a small maxStreamResponseSize, and legitimate block nodes sending normal-sized blocks have all their responses silently dropped, halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default maxSubscribeAttempts of 3 — combined with a block node pool of exactly 3 nodes — to deactivate all nodes simultaneously by sending 3 error responses to each node in parallel, causing a complete and immediate shutdown of block streaming with no fallback?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseTimeout (400ms) is used only for the server status request — not for the actual block streaming — to cause a scenario where status requests succeed but streaming connections hang indefinitely, consuming all available threads and preventing new block streaming attempts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the maxBlockItems check occurs in the append() method — which is called for every BlockItemSet — to craft a stream that sends many sets just below the limit, consuming excessive memory in the pending list before eventually triggering the exception, causing an OOM crash that halts the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that readmitDelay is applied uniformly to all block nodes — meaning all nodes deactivated at the same time will all become eligible for readmit at the same time — to create a synchronized deactivation/reactivation cycle that can be repeatedly exploited to maintain a near-continuous shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize has no lower bound constraint beyond @NotNull — allowing it to be set to DataSize.ofBytes(1) — to cause the gRPC channel to reject all real block responses as too large, while the importer continues attempting to connect to nodes that always fail, eventually deactivating all nodes and halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @Min(1) constraint on maxSubscribeAttempts is enforced by Jakarta Validation — which may not be applied in all code paths — to inject a value of 0 through a non-validated path, causing immediate deactivation of all block nodes on the first connection attempt?
